meaning: n.[植]石细胞,硬化细胞(亦作sclereide)
examples: Sclereid Any SCLERENCHYMA cell except the long fibers.
Sclerenchyma is often found associated with vascular tissue and exists as two distinct types of cell: the long FIBER and the shorter SCLEREID.
The results were as follows: Persimmon fruit consists of epidermis, sclereid layer and pulp tissues from outside to inside in the fruit transverse section.
